change_summary: EXP-263 integrated into WIN-001. detail_evidence grew from 6890 chars (c4) to 10631 chars (+3741). Three new strong-headed sub-paragraphs inserted between the spherical-waveguide-kernel paragraph and 'The dome's formula exposes the relabeling.' anchor: (1) 'Prediction or measurement?' frames Tesla's 11.78 Hz as apparatus-coherence parameter (US Patent 787412 wave-train coherence-time spec) and names Balser & Wagner 1960 as first Schumann measurement; (2) 'Tesla's actual Earth-resonance prediction was different' lists Tesla's separately-published ~6, 18, 30 Hz speculations (Colorado Springs Notes 1899-1900 via Tesla Universe transcription) and contrasts with the measured 7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz Schumann band; (3) 'Where the kernel sits: velocity correction' substitutes vacuum c for Tesla's 471,240 km/s in his round-trip-around-Earth formula and arrives at ~7.48 Hz, within ~5% of the measured Schumann fundamental, citing Schumann (1952). tldr_evidence rewritten 339->448 chars: drops obsolete 'globe measurement' phrasing, leads with apparatus-coherence framing, names 1960s first measurement, mentions Tesla's separately-predicted 6/18/30 Hz miss, preserves the version-history-circular punchline. No other field touched (claim, finding, verdict, detail_claim, detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, tldr_verdict, code_analysis all match c4 fingerprint exactly).
our_argument_summary: Refuted by Data verdict unchanged. EXP-263 strengthens the foundational distinction by spelling out three things the c4 text either implied or omitted: (a) Tesla's 11.78 Hz is the patent's apparatus coherence-time spec, not a measurement of Earth; (b) the measured Schumann band (7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz) was first observed in 1960 — long after Tesla's death, so the dome's 'predicted=observed' framing quotes one number twice; (c) Tesla's separately-published Earth-resonance estimates (~6, 18, 30 Hz) miss the measured band entirely. The kernel-of-truth paragraph credits Tesla with a near-correct order-of-magnitude framework (round-trip propagation across the Earth-ionosphere cavity) that, when corrected from his superluminal 1.572c to vacuum c, yields ~7.48 Hz — within ~5% of the measured Schumann fundamental — sharpening rather than weakening the verdict. The pre-EXP-263 structural arguments (formula requires Earth's diameter; v_a and r_disc are globe constants in disc clothing; live.html and wins.html collapse to the same Earth-sphere arithmetic; Colorado Springs location destroys flat-disc geometry; version-history-admitted circular reasoning; cross-WIN c-vs-v_a speed swap) are preserved verbatim.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py / pull_data.py touches WIN-001. Both derivation forms are static HTML. The live-power dashboard row displays hardcoded Predicted=11.788 / Observed=11.7869 with 1% tolerance. EXP-263 changed nothing about the dome side; it only sharpens our review's textual treatment of the prediction-vs-measurement distinction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded - both Predicted and Observed are static text restating the 1899/1905 Tesla figure.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 1.0% live-dashboard tolerance on a value Tesla wrote down to four decimals in 1905.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. EXP-263 is a textual amendment; the c4 dome-code analysis remains accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-001. No API call, no live fetch, no automated comparison logic. Confirmed by grep of monitor.py for '001', 'tesla', '11.78', '11.787' - no fetch line. The wins.html 'predicted=observed=11.787' is static text, and the live.html row is a hardcoded dashboard tile.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: v_a=1.574c is numerically within 0.08% of pi*c/2=1.5708c (the spherical-cavity fundamental-mode phase velocity); r_disc=20,015 km matches pi*R_earth=20,015.09 km to four decimals (Earth's half-circumference under the polar azimuthal-equidistant projection). The standard physics phenomenon - spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance - has been published since Schumann (1952). EXP-263 reinforces this by adding the velocity-correction kernel (Tesla's 1D round-trip at vacuum c gives c/circumference = 7.48 Hz, within 4.5% of the measured 7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tesla's US Patent 787412 (1905) and Colorado Springs Notes (1899) predate the dome model by over a century. Balser & Wagner (1960) measured the Schumann fundamental at 7.83 Hz decades after Tesla's death. The dome's 11.787 Hz target value is verbatim Tesla's wave-train-coherence reciprocal - verifiable in the patent text. The monitor script hardcodes the target on the same commit as any data-fetch line, so timestamping cannot establish prospective priority.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Neither dome derivation form uses a dome-intrinsic physical parameter. Wins.html: f=c/(2*disc_thickness) requires disc_thickness=12,717 km = Earth's diameter. Live.html: f=v_a/(2*r_disc) with v_a=1.574c (= pi*c/2 = spherical-cavity phase velocity, a globe constant) and r_disc=20,015 km (= pi*R_earth = Earth's half-circumference, a globe constant). The two forms collapse to the same Earth-sphere arithmetic (c4 hole #1 documented this). Firmament height H=9,086 km, sun altitude 5,733 km, moon altitude 2,534 km, and the aetheric scale length appear in neither form.

holes_found.description:
Cosmetic visual rendering bug: the integration of EXP-263 produced a four-
sequence (

tags. The EXP's after-string ended with two
tags AND the substring-replacement preserved the original anchor's leading two
tags, so they concatenated. Effect: a noticeably larger visual gap than the rest of the WIN at exactly the most rhetorically important transition point (between the kernel-of-truth credit and the formula-relabeling teardown).
holes_found.recommendation:
Strip the trailing

from the new content, leaving the original anchor's leading

intact. This is a substring patch on data/wins.json detail_evidence: replace 'every link.



The dome' with 'every link.

The dome'. Severity is minor - readers will likely not notice - but the fix is trivial and one of those visual tells that a careful dome defender could screenshot and frame as 'sloppy.' Worth the 30-second patch.

holes_found.description: The 'Tesla's actual Earth-resonance prediction was different' paragraph asserts specific values - 'approximately 6, 18, and 30 Hz' - sourced from the Tesla Universe transcription of the Colorado Springs Notes plus 'Wardenclyffe-era writings.' This is a secondary-source aggregation. A determined dome defender will go to primary text (the Colorado Springs Notes are public domain) and try to falsify the specific triplet. If Tesla's actual recorded resonance estimates are 'about 8 Hz' (some secondary sources) or '6-12 Hz fundamental' (others) rather than a clean 6/18/30 Hz odd-harmonic series, our citation is exposed and the structural argument ('Tesla's actual prediction missed the measured band') survives but loses a number we are quoting precisely. The analyst flagged this exact risk in EXP-263 anticipated_objections and curmudgeon_verification_recommended. The risk that Tesla wrote down NO clean 6/18/30 Hz triplet - and that the figures are an aggregation artifact - is non-zero.
holes_found.recommendation: EITHER (a) primary-source verify the 6/18/30 Hz figures against the Colorado Springs Notes 1899-1900 (Tesla Universe transcription page 62 ballpark) and add an inline page-anchored citation, OR (b) soften the wording: 'recorded fundamental and harmonic estimates in the single-digit-to-low-tens Hz range - none matching the measured 7.83/14.3/20.8 Hz Schumann band.' Option (b) preserves the structural argument unconditionally; option (a) preserves the rhetorical force of specific numbers if they reproduce. This is a moderate not minor hole because the paragraph's punchline ('Tesla's resonance prediction was off in both the fundamental and the harmonic spacing') depends on the specific 6/18/30 vs 7.83/14.3/20.8 mapping.

holes_found.description: The 'velocity correction' paragraph closes with 'The right physical result on a globe - which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured - is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity.' This is correct, but the preceding sentence ('Tesla's 11.78 Hz is therefore not a measurement of Earth at all; it is what you get when you take a near-correct order-of-magnitude estimate (round-trip propagation across the closed Earth-ionosphere cavity) and inflate it by pi/2 by substituting a phase-velocity speed for a time-of-flight speed') invites a careful reader to infer 'Tesla had the right formula at the wrong velocity, so 7.48 Hz at vacuum c IS the Schumann fundamental.' That inference is wrong: Tesla's 1D round-trip formula at vacuum c gives c/(2*pi*R) = 7.48 Hz, while the actual lossless Schumann fundamental from the spherical-cavity TM-mode formula is c*sqrt(2)/(2*pi*R) = 10.59 Hz, lowered to ~7.83 Hz by ~26% cavity loss. The 4.5% gap between Tesla's velocity-corrected 7.48 Hz and the measured 7.83 Hz is a coincidence between two different physical formulas (1D round-trip at c vs lossy 3D spherical cavity), not a velocity-only correction. The EXP-263 anticipated_objections document explicitly acknowledges this ('Cavity-loss correction would lower the resonance from a lossless cavity value, but you are going from 7.48 Hz UP to 7.83 Hz. That direction is wrong'); the published paragraph however does not.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-clause clarification at the close of the velocity-correction paragraph: '...The numerical proximity of Tesla's velocity-corrected 7.48 Hz to the measured 7.83 Hz reflects two different formulas (1D round-trip-at-c vs lossy-spherical-cavity TM-mode) happening to share c/(2*pi*R) as a leading factor - Tesla had the right order-of-magnitude scaling but neither the right dimensionality (1D vs 3D) nor the right velocity assumption.' Without this clause, an alert reader could derive an unintended 'Tesla had the right Earth-resonance formula' reading. With it, the kernel-of-truth credit narrows to 'right scaling, wrong everything else,' which is the operator's intended framing per the EXP. Not load-bearing - the existing 'order-of-magnitude estimate' phrasing already carries the warning - but the explicit acknowledgement closes the rhetorical loop.

holes_found.description: Two new DOI citations introduced in the 'Prediction or measurement?' and 'velocity correction' paragraphs warrant verification before they appear on the live site: (i) Balser & Wagner (1960) at doi:10.1029/JZ065i007p02103. The standard 'first Schumann observation' citation is Balser & Wagner (1960), but there are two co-published papers - one in Nature 188(4751), 638-641 (doi:10.1038/188638a0) and one in JGR 65 (the JZ065 DOI). The JGR DOI in the EXP is for J. Geophys. Res., vol. 65, issue 7, page 2103 - which corresponds to a Balser & Wagner JGR paper but I have not confirmed online from this session that this DOI resolves. (ii) Schumann (1952) at doi:10.1515/zna-1952-0202. The Zeitschrift fur Naturforschung A volume 7 page 149 paper is the canonical reference, and 10.1515/zna-1952-0202 is the deGruyter format for that issue, but again not independently confirmed.
holes_found.recommendation: Verify both DOIs resolve. If JGR 65 page 2103 is the wrong page (Balser & Wagner published multiple notes in JGR 65 - pp. 4081-4083 is also commonly cited), correct the DOI; otherwise keep. Schumann (1952) DOI is straightforward to confirm via doi.org. If either fails to resolve, swap to the Nature 1960 paper (10.1038/188638a0) for the measurement citation and a different bibliographic key for Schumann (e.g., Z. Naturforsch. 7a, 149 (1952)). Citation rigor matters because c2 review caught wrong DOIs in WIN-008/009/014 - the curmudgeon should not let new DOIs slip through unverified.

holes_found.description: TLDR change: the new tldr_evidence drops the old 'D=12,717 km Earth's diameter, not any dome parameter' geometric punchline in favor of the prediction-vs-measurement framing. This is a deliberate trade per the EXP rationale (TLDR sets up the foundational distinction; body carries the geometric arguments). However, the new TLDR no longer informs the casual reader that the dome's formula structurally requires a globe constant - that line of attack now appears only when the reader expands detail_evidence. For a critical review, the strongest 5-second take-away is 'the dome's number requires Earth's diameter as a parameter,' and that take-away is now hidden behind a click-to-expand.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: a 25-30 word addendum to the new TLDR could carry both punchlines: 'Tesla's 11.78 Hz isn't a measurement of Earth's resonance - it's the apparatus-coherence parameter from his 1905 patent... [existing text] ...and the formula needed to reproduce it (f=c/(2*D)) plugs in Earth's diameter, not any dome dimension.' This is a judgment call - the operator may prefer the cleaner three-sentence TLDR. Flagging for awareness, not as a required edit.

stronger_arguments.description: The velocity-correction kernel paragraph asserts a 5% gap between Tesla's velocity-corrected 7.48 Hz and the measured Schumann 7.83 Hz. The arithmetic is exact: 40,075/299,792.458 = 0.13367 s, 1/T = 7.481 Hz, gap to 7.83 = 4.46%. A stronger version of this paragraph would close by noting the lossless 3D spherical-cavity Schumann fundamental (c*sqrt(2)/(2*pi*R) = 10.59 Hz) is ABOVE Tesla's velocity-corrected 7.48 Hz by sqrt(2), and that the measured 7.83 Hz sits between them because cavity losses pull the 10.59 Hz down by ~26%. The proximity of Tesla's 7.48 Hz to the measured 7.83 Hz is a happy accident enabled by lossy-cavity physics that was not part of Tesla's reasoning. This sharpens 'Tesla had near-correct order of magnitude' to 'Tesla's order-of-magnitude scaling shares c/(2*pi*R) with the lossy-cavity result by coincidence,' which is the truer claim.
sources: Verified arithmetic (python3, c=299792.458 km/s, R=6371 km): c/(2*pi*R)=7.489 Hz; c*sqrt(2)/(2*pi*R)=10.591 Hz; 10.591/7.489=sqrt(2)=1.4142; (10.591-7.83)/10.591=26% (the canonical Schumann cavity-loss factor).

stronger_arguments.description: The 'Prediction or measurement?' paragraph cites Tesla's '0.08484 of a second' coherence-time quote from the patent. A stronger version would explicitly note the patent's structural form: Tesla wrote it as 'I have estimated to be not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second' - i.e., he proposed a transmitter design parameter (minimum wave-train duration), not an Earth-cavity measurement. The 'one-twelfth of a second' value is a designer's heuristic round number; the '0.08484' is the engineering-precision figure. Neither is a measurement. Highlighting Tesla's own 'estimated' wording makes the apparatus-vs-measurement distinction airtight.
sources: US Patent 787,412 specification text (already cited inline)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's wins.html cell reads 'Predicted 11.787 - Observed 11.787 - Exact match.' The EXP's TLDR rewrite implicitly acknowledges this is one number quoted twice, but the body could quote it directly. A single sentence in detail_evidence - 'The dome's predicted=observed=11.787 Hz cell pulls both columns from US Patent 787,412 paragraph 32; there is no independent observation' - would convert the 'apparatus-coherence' framing from analytical inference to citation-supported finding.
sources: US Patent 787,412 paragraph 32 (the wave-train coherence-time passage)

kernel_of_truth.description: Tesla genuinely was reaching for an Earth-cavity resonance phenomenon when he wrote down 11.78 Hz. His path concept (round-trip propagation around the Earth-ionosphere cavity) is qualitatively the right framework - Schumann (1952) and Balser & Wagner (1960) finished the work on the same physical setup. His velocity assumption (1.572c) is exactly the spherical-cavity fundamental-mode phase velocity (pi*c/2 = 1.5708c), a real and standard ionospheric-waveguide quantity. EXP-263 explicitly credits this with the velocity-correction paragraph and the cross-link to WIN-062. The credit is real and tightly scoped.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three structural failures remain: (a) the 11.78 Hz value Tesla wrote down is the patent's apparatus coherence-time spec, not an Earth-resonance prediction (Tesla's actual resonance estimates were elsewhere and missed the measured Schumann band); (b) the formula-form Tesla used (1D round-trip with phase-velocity speed) is not the right formula for the Schumann fundamental - the right formula is 3D spherical-cavity TM-mode with vacuum c, and the close numerical neighborhood between Tesla's 7.48 Hz and the measured 7.83 Hz is a coincidence enabled by lossy-cavity damping; (c) most importantly, the dome's WIN-001 isn't 'Tesla had a kernel of correct intuition' - it's 'Predicted 11.787 Hz, Observed 11.787 Hz, exact match' presented as flat-disc geometric evidence. Crediting Tesla's qualitative framework does not validate the dome's numerical-match claim or its flat-disc-geometry argument. The kernel sharpens the verdict; it does not save it.

advocate_mode.best_defense: After EXP-263 the strongest dome-defender play is: 'Your own kernel-of-truth paragraph admits Tesla had the right framework - round-trip propagation around the Earth-ionosphere cavity at a phase velocity that turns out to be the canonical spherical-cavity result. You are crediting Tesla with the physics and crediting the dome with relabeling Tesla. Why is that not crediting the dome with publishing a real Earth-cavity resonance number? Tesla's apparatus-vs-measurement distinction matters historiographically but does not change the fact that 11.78 Hz, when corrected for velocity, is in the Schumann band. The dome cites the value at face value and your own analysis says the value is in the right neighborhood. Verdict should be Standard Physics, not Refuted by Data.' Counter: the dome's claim is 'Predicted 11.787 Hz, Observed 11.787 Hz' (exact match), not 'Tesla was qualitatively right.' The dome's number is a globe constant divided by another globe constant; the exact match is one number quoted twice; and Tesla's velocity-corrected 7.48 Hz is not 11.78 Hz - it's a different number that happens to be near the actual measured Schumann fundamental. The defense conflates 'qualitative framework was right' with 'specific quantitative claim is confirmed,' and EXP-263 makes that conflation harder, not easier.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Hole #3 (lossless-vs-lossy clarification) and hole #5 (TLDR drop of geometric punchline) are the two surfaces where this defense gets traction. Either by itself is a minor refinement; both together would tighten the argument further. defense_survives stays at 2 (matches c4 baseline) - EXP-263 closes one open flank (apparatus-vs-measurement now explicit and sourced) but opens a small new one (kernel-of-truth credit could be misread as endorsement of Tesla's formula form).

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: EXP-263 is internally consistent with the c4 cross-WIN treatment. The new velocity-correction paragraph cross-links WIN-062 (1.574c claim) and is consistent with WIN-002 (Schumann 7.83 Hz overshoot from lossy-cavity damping) and WIN-029 (H=c/(4f) firmament-height back-derivation from Schumann). The cross-WIN c-vs-v_a speed-swap argument established in c4 is not weakened - the new paragraph endorses replacing v_a with c on the WIN-001 side, which lines up with the c4 hole-1 framing that 'WIN-001 survives only at v_a; WIN-029 survives only at c.' One subtle consistency check: does the new 'Tesla had the right framework once velocity is corrected' framing accidentally endorse WIN-029's H=c/(4f) form? No - H=c/(4f) is a 1/4-wave standing-wave formula (different dimensionality) and the Schumann fundamental is not a 1/4-wave mode of any cavity. The new content does not validate WIN-029's formula structure; it only credits Tesla's qualitative round-trip-around-Earth concept. Verified.

claims_checked: 1/0.08484 s = 11.785 Hz (claimed in new content; computed 1/0.08484 = 11.7869 - match to claimed precision; rounds to 11.785)
471,240 / 299,792.458 = 1.57189c (claimed 1.572 in new content - match to 4 figures)
40,075 km / c = 0.13367 s (claimed ~0.134 s in new content - match)
1 / 0.13367 s = 7.481 Hz (claimed ~7.48 Hz in new content - match)
Schumann band 7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz (claimed in new content - match to standard literature; e.g. Williams 1992 review gives 7.8/14/20/26/33 Hz)

Cross-check: 11.787 Hz / 7.83 Hz = 1.5054 (NOT 1.572). The pi/2 ratio is between Tesla's velocity (1.572c) and c, not between Tesla's frequency (11.787 Hz) and the Schumann fundamental (7.83 Hz). The new content does not claim the latter; checking only that no false equivalence is implied - it isn't.
quantitative_verification.computations: python3 with c=299,792.458 km/s, R_earth=6,371 km, equatorial_circumference=40,075 km. All published numbers in the new content match to rounding precision.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None in the published paragraphs. One framing risk (hole #3): the 7.48 Hz vs 7.83 Hz proximity is between two different formulas (Tesla 1D round-trip vs lossy 3D spherical cavity), and the published text leaves this implicit.

deeper_analysis: EXP-263 is a textually substantive amendment that closes the prediction-vs-measurement flank and adds a velocity-correction kernel. The integration is clean (no other field touched) and the new content is consistent with the c4 structural arguments. The biggest remaining hole is hole #2 (moderate): the specific 6/18/30 Hz Tesla-resonance numbers depend on a secondary-source aggregation that has not been primary-source verified, and a determined dome defender could exploit this. Hole #1 (minor visual rendering bug, 4-br tag sequence) is trivial to patch and worth the 30 seconds. Hole #3 (minor framing risk on the lossless-vs-lossy distinction) is optional polish. Hole #4 (DOI verification) is standard hygiene. Hole #5 (TLDR loses geometric punchline) is a judgment call. defense_survives stays at 2 - EXP-263 closes one open flank and opens a small new one, net neutral on the adversarial-strength score. Verdict: Refuted by Data, unchanged. Recommended action: minor_edit (the 4-br patch + ideally the 6/18/30 Hz softening or primary-source check). The other items are optional.

citations_verified: US Patent 787,412 (already cited and verified in c4) - re-cited inline in new 'Prediction or measurement?' paragraph; quote 'wave or wave-train should continue for a certain interval of time, which I have estimated to be not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second' is verbatim from the patent
Schumann band frequencies 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz (match standard literature)
citations_unchecked: Balser & Wagner (1960) doi:10.1029/JZ065i007p02103 - DOI format is JGR-style (vol 65, issue 7, page 2103). Plausible match for the JGR Balser & Wagner paper on Earth-ionosphere cavity resonances, but I did not resolve the DOI online from this session. Recommend verification.

Schumann (1952) doi:10.1515/zna-1952-0202 - DOI format matches deGruyter Z. Naturforsch. for vol 7 issue 2. Plausible match for Schumann's 1952 paper on Earth-ionosphere cavity resonances, but I did not resolve the DOI online from this session. Recommend verification.

Tesla Universe transcription URL (https://teslauniverse.com/nikola-tesla/books/nikola-tesla-colorado-springs-notes-1899-1900) - URL format is plausible; primary-source spot-check of the 6/18/30 Hz figures recommended (see hole #2).
